goal:
Voice Tutor claim extraction module.

GOAL: a pure module (claims.py at the voice-tutor repo root) that decomposes a document's text into a set of discrete, assessable claims — the "rubric" that both conversation steering and future scoring will consume.

CONTRACT REQUIREMENTS
- Pure function core: document text in -> structured claim list out. Each claim has: an id, the claim text, and a source anchor (the supporting quote/passage from the document). No Pipecat, no bot.py, no app.py wiring, no UI.
- Persistence: a write helper that caches the claim set as a human-readable sidecar next to the document, mirroring the existing documents.py summary sidecar pattern (which writes DOCUMENTS_DIR / f"{doc_id}.summary.txt"). Here write DOCUMENTS_DIR / f"{doc_id}.claims.json", generated once per doc.
- Determinism strategy for tests: the LLM call must be mocked (or golden-fixture outputs used) — name the chosen approach in the contract. The hermetic suite MUST pass without any network access.
- Fixtures: use the real documents provided in test fixtures (see OPERATIONAL CONSTRAINTS below).
- Claim granularity is a known open judgment call — target claims a person could plausibly articulate in 1-3 spoken sentences; not one claim per sentence of the doc, not chapter-level themes. A typical doc should yield roughly 10-40 claims.

OUT OF SCOPE: scoring transcripts against claims, steering prompts, any live-session integration.

OPERATIONAL CONSTRAINTS (verifier environment — read carefully)
- The verifier runs EXACTLY this command in a fresh git worktree that has NO local .venv:
    uv run --with pytest pytest tests/test_claims.py -q
  Therefore ALL hermetic tests for this work must live in the single file tests/test_claims.py.
- The `anthropic` SDK IS available in the environment (voice-tutor depends on pipecat-ai[anthropic]); `import anthropic` resolves. `pytest` is supplied via `--with pytest`. Do NOT add pytest to pyproject.toml.
- claims.py must import only the Anthropic SDK and the standard library. It must NOT import bot.py, app.py, pipecat, fastapi, or any heavy runtime dependency — keep the module's import closure minimal so the verifier env stays satisfiable.
- Any real LLM call (Anthropic client) must be mocked/patched in the tests. The suite must pass with no API key set and no network. Do not read an API key at import time.
- FIXTURES: copy these three REAL documents into committed in-repo test fixtures (e.g. under tests/fixtures/claims/), and have the tests read those in-repo committed copies. The tests must NOT read from the ~/.voice-tutor/documents path at test time (that would be non-hermetic and machine-specific). Source files to copy in:
    ~/.voice-tutor/documents/12f379a0-5a04-4eb6-b349-1c3c0690fe17.txt
    ~/.voice-tutor/documents/8050fe28-f897-4947-953d-7ca38fd2e0ad.txt
    ~/.voice-tutor/documents/a9f59a8f-7d39-48c3-ba66-14e3b8c8d8c6.txt
- Persistence tests must write the .claims.json sidecar into a pytest tmp_path (redirecting DOCUMENTS_DIR the way tests/conftest.py already does for documents.py), never into the real ~/.voice-tutor/documents directory.

DEFINITION OF DONE (behavioral)
- claims.py exposes a pure decomposition function (document text -> list of {id, claim, source anchor}) and a write helper that persists the claim set to <doc_id>.claims.json next to the doc, generated once per doc.
- tests/test_claims.py passes hermetically via `uv run --with pytest pytest tests/test_claims.py -q` with no network and no API key, exercising the real fixture documents for the decomposition path (LLM mocked) and a tmp_path-redirected sidecar write for the persistence path.
title: claim extraction module

sprints.title: pure decomposition core
sprints.description: Create claims.py at the voice-tutor repo root exposing a pure decomposition function: document text in -> ordered list of discrete claim records out, each record having a stable id, the claim text, and a source anchor (the supporting quote/passage from the document). Import ONLY the anthropic SDK and the standard library — do NOT import bot.py, app.py, pipecat, fastapi, or any heavy runtime dep, and do NOT read an API key at import time (construct/use the Anthropic client lazily inside the call). Define the Anthropic prompt so it targets the required granularity: claims a person could articulate in 1-3 spoken sentences (not one-per-sentence, not chapter themes), yielding ~10-40 claims for a typical doc; parse the model's structured response into the claim records with deterministic ids and validated shape. In tests/test_claims.py (the ONLY hermetic test file, since the verifier runs `uv run --with pytest pytest tests/test_claims.py -q`), copy the three real source documents from ~/.voice-tutor/documents/{12f379a0-5a04-4eb6-b349-1c3c0690fe17, 8050fe28-f897-4947-953d-7ca38fd2e0ad, a9f59a8f-7d39-48c3-ba66-14e3b8c8d8c6}.txt into committed in-repo fixtures under tests/fixtures/claims/, and have tests read those committed copies (never the ~/.voice-tutor path). Patch/mock the Anthropic client so the suite passes with no network and no API key; assert on the parsed structure (ids present/unique, claim text non-empty, source anchor is a substring/passage of the fixture text) driven by the real fixture documents. This sprint ships a working, tested pure core.

sprints.title: sidecar persistence + generate-once
sprints.description: Add a write helper to claims.py that persists the claim set to DOCUMENTS_DIR / f"{doc_id}.claims.json" next to the document, mirroring the existing documents.py summary-sidecar pattern (which writes DOCUMENTS_DIR / f"{doc_id}.summary.txt"), producing human-readable JSON. Enforce generated-once-per-doc semantics: if the .claims.json sidecar already exists it is reused rather than regenerated (no redundant LLM call). Reference DOCUMENTS_DIR the same way documents.py does so tests can redirect it. Extend tests/test_claims.py: redirect DOCUMENTS_DIR into pytest tmp_path (using the same conftest.py mechanism the repo already uses for documents.py) so writes land in tmp_path and NEVER in the real ~/.voice-tutor/documents directory; with the Anthropic call still mocked, assert the sidecar is written to the expected tmp_path filename, that its JSON round-trips back into the same claim records, and that a second generate call for an already-cached doc reuses the sidecar without invoking the (mocked) LLM again. This sprint ships tested persistence and caching, completing the behavioral Definition of Done.
